commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
Adds a dry-run-by-default label color migration script and updates the label sync script’s default label-family colors.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is repository administration tooling, not a product bug. The relevant verification path is the PR’s dry-run output plus source inspection of all label-creation paths.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can align the workflow’s size-label creation color with the PR’s new palette while leaving final label-policy approval to maintainers.

target.title: chore(labels): add label color sync policy
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2-5 bullets:

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

- Problem: OpenClaw label colors currently use saturated colors for routine taxonomy labels, so channels, plugins, apps, areas, impacts, priorities, and automation gates compete visually.
- Why it matters: Maintainers scanning large issue and PR lists need saturated colors to mean urgency or action, not ordinary categorization.
- What changed: Added `scripts/sync-openclaw-label-colors.mjs`, a dry-run-by-default GitHub label color migration script that updates colors only, preserves label names/descriptions, and reports changes by label family. Also updated `scripts/sync-labels.ts` so future labels created from `.github/labeler.yml` inherit the quieter family palette.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not edit live labels by itself, rename labels, change descriptions, or alter labeler path rules.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

External contributors must show after-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw setup. Unit tests, mocks, lint, typechecks, snapshots, and CI are supplemental only. Screenshots are encouraged even for CLI, console, text, or log changes; terminal screenshots and copied live output count. Be mindful of private information like IP addresses, API keys, phone numbers, non-public endpoints, or other private details when providing evidence.

- Behavior or issue addressed: Label color strategy can be reviewed and applied without renaming labels or changing descriptions, and future labels created by repo tooling use the same family palette.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout with GitHub CLI authenticated to `openclaw/openclaw`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/sync-openclaw-label-colors.mjs`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Dry run completed and reported `Would update 116 labels.` Family summary: app 4, channel 25, clawsweeper 10, extension 16, impact 2, plugin 3, priority 1, proof 2, resolution 8, risk 1, size 5, specific 26, triage-candidate 10, triage-status 3.
- Observed result after fix: The script generated the expected color-only migration plan, grouped changes by label family, and did not mutate live labels without `--apply`.
- What was not tested: The live `--apply` mutation was not run from this PR branch.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Live labels showed saturated blue/purple/green colors across routine taxonomy labels such as `channel:*`, `app:*`, `extensions:*`, and area labels.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.

- Root cause: N/A
- Missing detection / guardrail: N/A
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: N/A
- Scenario the test should lock in: N/A
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: N/A
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A
- If no new test is added, why not: Script-only maintainer migration utility; validated with syntax, formatter, lint, diff, and live dry-run checks.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None until a maintainer runs `node scripts/sync-openclaw-label-colors.mjs --apply`.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

```text
Before:
[maintainer scans issue list] -> [routine taxonomy labels compete with priority/risk colors]

After:
[maintainer runs script --apply] -> [taxonomy labels become quiet] -> [priority/risk/action labels stand out]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: The new script calls GitHub through the existing authenticated `gh` CLI. It defaults to dry-run and only mutates colors when explicitly run with `--apply`.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node.js v24.15.0
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): GitHub CLI against `openclaw/openclaw`
- Relevant config (redacted): Existing authenticated `gh` session

### Steps

1. Run `node --check scripts/sync-openclaw-label-colors.mjs`.
2. Run `node --experimental-strip-types --check scripts/sync-labels.ts`.
3. Run `pnpm exec oxfmt --check scripts/sync-openclaw-label-colors.mjs scripts/sync-labels.ts`.
4. Run `pnpm exec oxlint scripts/sync-openclaw-label-colors.mjs scripts/sync-labels.ts --deny-warnings`.
5. Run `git diff --check`.
6. Run `node scripts/sync-openclaw-label-colors.mjs`.

### Expected

- The scripts parse, are formatted, have no lint or whitespace errors, and report color-only label changes in dry-run mode.

### Actual

- All narrow validation passed.
- Dry run reported `Would update 116 labels.`

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Script syntax, formatting, lint, whitespace, future label default colors, and GitHub dry-run label inventory query.
- Edge cases checked: The migration script preserves names/descriptions by only invoking `gh label edit --color`; no `--name` or `--description` mutation is used.
- What you did **not** verify: Live `--apply` label mutation.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Running the script with `--apply` changes many repository label colors at once.
  - Mitigation: The script defaults to dry-run, prints every planned color change grouped by family, and only updates colors while preserving names and descriptions.
target.author: takhoffman

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
